{"action":"created","issue":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/numpy/numpy/issues/27618","repository_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/numpy/numpy","labels_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/numpy/numpy/issues/27618/labels{/name}","comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/numpy/numpy/issues/27618/comments","events_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/numpy/numpy/issues/27618/events","html_url":"https://github.com/numpy/numpy/issues/27618","id":2606014419,"node_id":"I_kwDOAA3dP86bVJ_T","number":27618,"title":"BUG: TypeError in np.array_equal with equal_nan=True for string Arrays ","user":{"login":"monikasingh8097","id":25644713,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjI1NjQ0NzEz","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/25644713?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/monikasingh8097","html_url":"https://github.com/monikasingh8097","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/monikasingh8097/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/monikasingh8097/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/monikasingh8097/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/monikasingh8097/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/monikasingh8097/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/monikasingh8097/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/monikasingh8097/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/monikasingh8097/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/monikasingh8097/received_events","type":"User","user_view_type":"public","site_admin":false},"labels":[{"id":6359214,"node_id":"MDU6TGFiZWw2MzU5MjE0","url":"https://api.github.com/repos/numpy/numpy/labels/00%20-%20Bug","name":"00 - Bug","color":"fef2c0","default":false,"description":null}],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":2,"created_at":"2024-10-22T17:04:18Z","updated_at":"2024-10-23T06:55:49Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"NONE","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"### Describe the issue:

When using np.array_equal with equal_nan=True to compare string arrays containing np.nan, a TypeError is raised. This is unexpected behavior, as np.nan should be treated as a valid value for comparison in string arrays . 

### Reproduce the code example:

```python
import numpy as np
a1 = np.array(['bug', 'test',np.nan])   
a2 = np.array(['bug', 'test',np.nan])  
result = np.array_equal(a1, a2, equal_nan=True)  
print(result) # Error

import numpy as np
a1 = np.array(['bug', 'test',np.nan])   
a2 = np.array(['bug', 'test',np.nan])  
result = np.array_equal(a1, a2, equal_nan=False)  
print(result) # True
```


### Error message:

```shell
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "/home/monik/bug/./new_error.py", line 5, in <module>
    result = np.array_equal(a1, a2, equal_nan=True)  
             ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
  File "/home/monik/bug/test-env/lib/python3.11/site-packages/numpy/_core/numeric.py", line 2569, in array_equal
    a1nan, a2nan = isnan(a1), isnan(a2)
                   ^^^^^^^^^
TypeError: ufunc 'isnan' not supported for the input types, and the inputs could not be safely coerced to any supported types according to the casting rule ''safe''
```


### Python and NumPy Versions:

2.1.2
3.11.9 (main, Apr 27 2024, 21:16:11) [GCC 13.2.0]

I have checked this issue in the older version of NumPy (1.25.0) got the same error.

### Runtime Environment:

_No response_

When we mix np.nan (a float) with strings in a numpy array, the array's data type becomes object, which causes comparison difficulties with np.nan. We will need to write a custom array_equal() function to tackle this type of arrays. Please assign this issue to me I will come up with a custom array_equal function to handle this.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/numpy/numpy/issues/comments/2431075598/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"performed_via_github_app":null}}
